title: <div> * Key Columns to Monitor: Date of Request, Job Name, Item(s) Needed, Urgen
content:
* Key Columns to Monitor: Date of Request, Job Name, Item(s) Needed, Urgency.
C. Workflow for Equipment Requests:
* Employee Submission: At the end of their workday (during the check-out process, see below), employees access the Google Form and submit their equipment/supply requests for the following day.
* Daily Review (You/Your Dad):
* Morning (First Thing): You or your father (or a designated person) review the "Master Request Log" (Google Sheet).
* Prioritize: Filter by "Urgency" and "Job Name." Address "Urgent" or "Emergency" requests immediately.
* Consolidate: Combine requests for the same items from different jobs if possible for bulk purchasing.
* Assign Tasks: Determine who will pick up which items (e.g., "John picks up plumbing supplies for Job A," "Dad picks up lumber for Job B").
* Procurement: Assigned individuals go to the supply stores with a consolidated list.
* Delivery/Pick-up: Items are delivered to the job site or made available for pick-up at the warehouse as requested.
* Status Updates (Optional but Recommended): In the Google Sheet, consider adding columns like:
context: From document: OK, I have been taken in to my father‘s company, Majestic design. It’s… — chunk 18 (chunk 18/22)
